nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of lagoon and seaward reefs at depths of roughly 1–60 m; adults often forage in groups of around 20 individuals

decorPreferences: open swimming space along the length of the tank
long footprint (5–6 ft) preferred over tall depth

diet.notes: Primarily grazes filamentous and macroalgae from live rock; dried marine seaweed (nori) and spirulina-based preparations are generally advised at least three times per week. Has been documented accepting flake/pellet foods and occasional meaty offerings such as mysis shrimp in mixed-community feedings. A vitamin-rich diet (including periodic vitamin C supplementation) is commonly recommended to reduce risk of Head and Lateral Line Erosion.

summary: A hardy, oval-bodied Indo-Pacific surgeonfish prized for its efficient algae grazing and relatively peaceful demeanour toward non-tang tankmates. Generally considered a good entry-level Zebrasoma; have been known to be aggressive toward other Zebrasoma tangs.
careNotes:
The Scopas Tang (Zebrasoma scopas) tends to reach roughly 20–30 cm in well-kept aquariums; FishBase records a maximum of 40 cm SL in the wild, though aquarium specimens are commonly reported at 20–30 cm. A spacious tank around 500 L (5–6 ft footprint) is generally advised — sources span 120 gal (454 L) at the entry-level end to substantially larger systems for adults, and the species is an active swimmer that has been reported to pace and develop stress-related issues in undersized tanks.

Water quality should be stable and well-oxygenated within standard reef parameters (SG ~1.022–1.026, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12). Temperature is commonly recommended around 23–27°C, though source ranges span 22°C (LiveAquaria's 72°F lower bound) to 28°C (FishBase's modeled mean ~27.6°C).

Diet should lean heavily on marine algae; offering dried seaweed and spirulina-based foods at least three times per week is generally advised. Vitamin C supplementation (a weekly drop on food) is commonly suggested to reduce risk of Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE), which this species is known to develop in poor water quality or on a vitamin-deficient diet. The species is generally considered reef-safe; well-fed individuals tend not to pick at corals.

Like all Zebrasoma tangs, this species is highly aggressive toward conspecifics and other Zebrasoma — best kept singly per tank unless the system is very large and pairs are introduced simultaneously. It is generally peaceable with non-Zebrasoma reef fish once it establishes its place in the tank's pecking order.

Acanthurids possess a sharp caudal-peduncle blade (the 'scalpel') used for defense; it is not venomous but can inflict lacerations on handlers and tankmates during conflicts. Handle with care during transfers.

This species is a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite, common among acanthurids. Wild observations suggest both pair-spawning and group-spawning aggregations occur, with some reports of facultative monogamy. Captive breeding has not been documented in available hobby sources.

Source cross-check notes: Adult size varied — FishBase 40 cm SL (wild maximum), LiveAquaria 12 in (30 cm), Reef Chasers 12 in (30 cm), ReefApp 20 cm, Reefs.com 8 in (20 cm); used a 20–30 cm aquarium-realistic range. Minimum tank ranged 120 gal (454 L) to 130 gal (492 L) at retail to 250 L at ReefApp; per the tank-size cautious-larger rule rounded up to 500 L to accommodate the active swimming behaviour repeatedly emphasised in prose. Temperature spread 22–28°C across sources; averaged to 23–27°C.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been documented in available hobby sources. In the wild, Zebrasoma scopas exhibits facultative monogamy and forms resident spawning aggregations with both pair-spawning and group-spawning observed (FishBase). Spawning in aquaria is considered highly unlikely given tank-size and pair-bonding constraints.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page for Scopas Tang. Provided max size (12 in), tank minimum (130 gal), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.020–1.025), dKH (8–12), care level (moderate), diet (herbivore, marine seaweed/algae 3x/week), temperament (semi-aggressive), and reef compatibility (yes).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked species identity, family (Acanthuridae) and order (Acanthuriformes), max size (40 cm SL), depth (0–60 m, usually 1–60 m), native range (Indo-Pacific: East Africa to Tuamotu Islands, southern Japan to Lord Howe and Rapa), preferred temperature (24.6–28.9°C, mean ~27.6°C), IUCN status (Least Concern, 2010), and reproductive biology (monogamous facultative/social, resident spawning aggregations).

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size (20 cm), minimum tank (250 L), SG (~1.026), reef-safe status, diet (macro/microalgae, supplemental small crustaceans), temperament (aggressive toward similar species, otherwise peaceful), need for hiding places and open swimming space.

additional.name: Reef Chasers Care Guide
additional.notes: Designated Reef2Reef wiki secondary returned HTTP 403; substituted this hobby guide. Confirmed adult size (~12 in), minimum tank (120 gal / 454 L), aggressive only toward other Zebrasoma tangs and otherwise peaceful, HLLE susceptibility, use of caudal spine as defensive weapon, herbivorous grazing diet requiring live rock.

fish.breedingNotes: No captive breeding records found. Wild populations form resident spawning aggregations with facultative monogamy; pair- and group-spawning both observed. Not bred in the hobby due to tank-size and pair-bonding constraints.
